'''Trent Merrin''' (born 7 October 1989) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a {{rlp|lk|pr}} and {{rlp|sr}} forward for most of his playing career in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]].

He played for the [[St. George Illawarra Dragons]], with whom he won the [[2010 NRL Grand Final]], and the [[Penrith Panthers]] in the [[National Rugby League]]. He also spent a season with the [[Leeds Rhinos]] in the [[Super League]]. Merrin has also played at representative level for [[Country New South Wales rugby league team|NSW Country]], [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]], [[Australia national rugby league team|Australia]], [[NRL All Stars team|NRL All Stars]] and the [[Prime Minister's XIII]] sides.

==Background==
Merrin was born in [[Sydney, New South Wales, Australia]].  Merrin originally played junior rugby league with [[Marrickville]] RSL while living in [[Sydenham, New South Wales|Sydenham]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Massoud |first=Josh |date=2012-07-17 |title=South Sydney Snub Fites Up Young Dragon Trent Merrin |work=[[The Advertiser (Adelaide)|The Advertiser]] |url=http://www.adelaidenow.com.au/sport/nrl/south-sydney-snub-fires-up-young-dragon-trent-merrin/news-story/e936105d67e101116d1b21029ad1e36c?sv=11af7f245501c3d7ff341b8a55a3ed59 |access-date=2017-04-30 |via=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]]}}{{dead link |date=July 2021 |fix-attempted=yes}}</ref> Merrin then moved with his family in his early teens from Sydney to the [[Illawarra]]. He then played rugby league with the [[Port Kembla Blacks]] and the [[Shellharbour Sharks]]. Merrin claims another Shellharbour legend, [[Australian national rugby league team|Australian]] representative [[Luke Bailey (rugby league)|Luke Bailey]], was "one of [his] idols growing up".<ref>{{Cite news |last=Chammas |first=Michael |date=2011-03-10 |title=Luke Bailey is Trent Merrin's target |work=Illawarra Mercury |url=https://www.illawarramercury.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/luke-bailey-is-trent-merrins-target/2100140.aspx |url-status=live |access-date=2015-10-11 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20160817072442/http://www.illawarramercury.com.au/story/634320/luke-bailey-is-trent-merrins-target/ |archive-date=2016-08-17}}</ref>

==Playing career==
In 2008, Merrin played for [[St. George Illawarra]] under-20s team in the [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|Toyota Cup]], scoring two tries for the season. He was selected as St. George Illawarra's Toyota Cup Player of the Year in 2008.{{cn|date=May 2025}}

In 2009, he was selected to play first grade for St. George Illawarra, being chosen on the interchange bench.  He made his first grade debut for the club in round 12 of the [[2009 NRL season]] against [[Penrith Panthers|Penrith]] at [[WIN Stadium]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2009-03-31 |title=Merrin named for NRL debut |url=http://www.dragons.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=11591 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110706104039/http://www.dragons.com.au/default.aspx?s=article-display&id=11591 |archive-date=2011-07-06 |access-date=2015-10-11 |website=[[St. George Illawarra Dragons]]}}</ref> He made three appearances in his debut season for St. George Illawarra as the club won the [[Minor Premiership]] but lost both matches in the finals series.

He was a prominent squad member of the 2010 premiership-winning St. George Illawarra team, and came from the bench to play in the [[2010 NRL Grand Final]]. In May 2011, he was selected on the bench for [[New South Wales rugby league team|New South Wales]] for game 1 of the [[2011 State of Origin series]]. He was selected for the second match but was then dropped for the third. On 2 March 2015, Merrin signed a five-year contract with the [[Penrith Panthers]], starting in 2016.<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-03-02 |title=Trent Merrin signs with Panthers |url=http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/trent-merrin-signs-with-panthers-16563/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150421145229/http://www.zerotackle.com/nrl/trent-merrin-signs-with-panthers-16563/ |archive-date=2015-04-21 |access-date=2015-10-11 |website=Zero Tackle}}</ref>

On 15 September 2015, it was announced that Merrin would captain a representative team for the first time. On 26 September 2015, he led the [[Prime Minister's XIII]] in their [[Prime Minister's XIII#2015|2015 clash]] with [[Papua New Guinea national rugby league team|Papua New Guinea]].<ref>{{Cite web |date=2015-09-15 |title=Merrin to lead Prime Minister's XIII |url=http://www.nrl.com/merrin-to-lead-prime-ministers-xiii/tabid/10874/newsid/89831/default.aspx |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20151015032500/http://www.nrl.com/merrin-to-lead-prime-ministers-xiii/tabid/10874/newsid/89831/default.aspx |archive-date=2015-10-15 |access-date=2015-10-11 |website=[[National Rugby League]]}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |date=2015-09-15 |title=Trent Merrin named as Prime Minister's XIII captain for Papua New Guinea clash |work=ABC News |publisher=[[Australian Broadcasting Corporation]] |url=http://www.abc.net.au/news/2015-09-15/trent-merrin-named-as-pm-xiii-captain-for-papua-new-guinea-clash/6778306 |url-status=live |access-date=2015-04-27 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20150915210014/http://www.abc.net.au/news/2015-09-15/trent-merrin-named-as-pm-xiii-captain-for-papua-new-guinea-clash/6778306 |archive-date=2015-09-15}}</ref>

Merrin was a part of the Penrith sides which made the finals series in 2016, 2017 and 2018 bowing out at the second week on each occasion.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Chammas |first=Michael |date=2016-09-18 |title=NRL finals 2016: Canberra Raiders vs Penrith Panthers {{!}} PHOTOS, REPORT |work=[[Illawarra Mercury]] |url=https://www.illawarramercury.com.au/story/4172172/nrl-finals-2016-canberra-raiders-defeat-penrith-panthers-photos-report/}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |last=Lutton |first=Phil |url=https://www.smh.com.au/sport/nrl/nrl-finals-2017-penrith-panthers-end-season-on-a-frustrating-night-as-brisbane-broncos-prepare-to-meet-melbourne-storm-20170915-gyim71.html/ |title=NRL Finals 2017: Penrith Panthers end season on a frustrating night as Brisbane Broncos prepare to meet Melbourne Storm |date=2017-09-15 |work=Sydney Morning Herald }}</ref><ref>{{Cite web |last=Newton |first=Alicia |date=2018-10-25 |title=Penrith Panthers 2018 season review |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/19/penrith-panthers-2018-season-review/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20180919033952/https://www.nrl.com/news/2018/09/19/penrith-panthers-2018-season-review/ |archive-date=2018-09-19 |website=[[National Rugby League]]}}</ref>

After departing Penrith, Merrin signed a contract to join English [[Super League]] team [[Leeds Rhinos|Leeds]]. After just one season with Leeds, it was announced that Merrin was returning to Australia on compassionate grounds. On 29 November 2019 it was revealed that Merrin had signed a two-year deal with St. George Illawarra.<ref>{{Cite news |date=2019-11-29 |title=Matt Prior: Leeds Rhinos sign forward as captain Trent Merrin leaves on compassionate grounds |work=BBC Sport |publisher=[[British Broadcasting Corporation]] |url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/50607005}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |title=Rugby League news: Toronto Wolfpack tie down ten, Jarrod Sammut on the move, Trent Merrin 'completes St George switch' |work=[[Hull Daily Mail]] |url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-live-transfer-news-3590465/ |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20191130190615/https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/rugby-league-live-transfer-news-3590465 |archive-date=2019-11-30}}</ref>

On 21 April 2021, Merrin announced his retirement from rugby league effective immediately.<ref>{{Cite web |last=Walter |first=Brad |date=2021-04-21 |title='Selfless' Merrin calls time to make way for new breed of Dragons |url=https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/04/21/dragon-heart-merrin-announces-immediate-retirement/ |url-status=live |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210421075542/https://www.nrl.com/news/2021/04/21/dragon-heart-merrin-announces-immediate-retirement/ |archive-date=2021-04-21 |access-date=2021-04-21 |website=[[National Rugby League]]}}</ref>

==Personal life==
Merrin was engaged to world-class surfer [[Sally Fitzgibbons]].<ref>{{Cite news |last=Walshaw |first=Nick |date=2015-04-16 |title=Dragons star Trent Merrin on his NRL career, Sally Fitzgibbons, the big rep – nothing has come easy |work=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]] |url=http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/dragons-star-trent-merrin-on-his-nrl-career-sally-fitzgibbons-the-big-rep-nothing-has-come-easy/news-story/04322e843be0e05f89ec080b26fbd2d3 |url-status=live |access-date=2017-06-20 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170828102303/http://www.dailytelegraph.com.au/sport/nrl/dragons-star-trent-merrin-on-his-nrl-career-sally-fitzgibbons-the-big-rep-nothing-has-come-easy/news-story/04322e843be0e05f89ec080b26fbd2d3 |archive-date=2017-08-28}}</ref>

Merrin was in a long-term relationship with Jessica Watson and the couple had their first son in March 2020 and their second son in November 2021.<ref>{{Cite news |last=Coates |first=Sally |date=2020-05-19 |title=Babies, engagements fill the NRL's COVID-19 shutdown period |work=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]] |via=[[The Courier-Mail]] |url-status=live |archive-date=5 November 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20251105001104/https://www.couriermail.com.au/entertainment/confidential/babies-engagements-fill-the-nrls-covid19-shutdown-period/news-story/85630e6a2b244bf4a5834c4cbbde9217?amp&nk=492ba0bff213dfba9017fada829c0ec6-1762301475 |at=Let's get Merrin-ed |url=https://www.qt.com.au/news/nrls-shutdown-babies-and-engagements-as-players-ke/4019261/ |url-access=subscription |access-date=2020-06-04}}</ref>{{better source needed|date=November 2025|reason=This source only pertains to their child's birth in 2020.}}

On 4 November 2025, it was reported that Merrin had been arrested and charged by [[New South Wales Police Force|police in New South Wales]] due to his alleged theft of [[cryptocurrency]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.smh.com.au/national/nsw/former-nrl-player-trent-merrin-charged-with-alleged-140-000-cryptocurrency-theft-20251104-p5n7rq.html|access-date=5 November 2025|work=[[The Sydney Morning Herald]] |url-status=live |date=4 November 2025 |archive-url=https://archive.today/20251104085335/https://www.smh.com.au/national/nsw/former-nrl-player-trent-merrin-charged-with-alleged-140-000-cryptocurrency-theft-20251104-p5n7rq.html |archive-date=4 November 2025 |title=Former NRL player Trent Merrin charged with alleged $140,000 cryptocurrency theft |first=Daniel |last=Lo Surdo}}</ref>
